skill.name: imagegen-frontend-web
skill.description: Creates premium frontend image references for websites, landing pages, and product pages. It directs one horizontal image per section with consistent art direction, layout variety, and conversion-aware structure.
skill.summary: Generate premium section-by-section frontend design reference images.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown prose only. The backtick alerts are inline formatting, and the crypto and reconnaissance alerts are ordinary design vocabulary. No malicious intent, prompt-injection attempt against the audit, data exfiltration, or executable behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Parsed as Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 81-95 contain inline markdown labels that explain design scale values. They do not invoke Ruby, shell commands, scripts, or user-controlled execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are markdown backticks around plain explanatory text. No executable command syntax, interpreter call, or variable interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Words Parsed as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain words such as design, default, moodboard, mid, and gradient. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or security decision appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are ordinary prose in a frontend art-direction guide. There is no code path that selects DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any other cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Layout Vocabulary Parsed as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system and network reconnaissance alerts match ordinary design instructions about layout, spacing, sections, and visual rhythm. No host, user, process, port, or network discovery behavior is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a markdown-only image direction skill with no scripts or commands. The flagged words are contextual design vocabulary, not reconnaissance instructions.

content.value_statement: Generic AI website mockups often repeat the same safe layouts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward section-by-section image references with stronger art direction.
actual_capabilities: Generates one separate horizontal reference image for each requested website section.
Defaults ambiguous requests to practical section counts for heroes, landing pages, websites, products, and portfolios.
Selects varied hero compositions, section anchors, background modes, and CTA styles.
Maintains one consistent palette, typography system, image treatment, and brand world across all section images.
Applies clarity checks for hierarchy, spacing, image usage, conversion path, and implementation readability.
Provides structured art-direction rules for SaaS, editorial, agency, e-commerce, luxury, and minimalist briefs.
limitations: It creates visual reference direction, not production frontend code.
Multi-section requests can require many image generations and may increase cost or latency.
Output quality depends on the user brief and the available image generation model.
It does not test accessibility, responsiveness, or browser behavior after implementation.

use_cases.title: Create a Landing Page Concept
use_cases.description: Generate a coherent set of section images for a new marketing page before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Brief a Frontend Build
use_cases.description: Produce image references that show hierarchy, spacing, CTA priority, and reusable visual components.

use_cases.title: Explore Campaign Art Direction
use_cases.description: Test premium visual directions across hero, proof, features, pricing, and final CTA sections.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Website Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a simple but polished landing page concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a premium landing page for a productivity app. Generate 6 horizontal section images, one per section, with a consistent palette.

prompt_templates.title: Product Page System
prompt_templates.scenario: You need image-led references for an e-commerce or product page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 6-section product page for a high-end desk lamp. Use strong product imagery, varied section layouts, and clear purchase CTAs.

prompt_templates.title: SaaS Marketing Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a conversion-aware SaaS page with proof and pricing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 8 horizontal images for a fintech SaaS website. Include hero, trust bar, features, product showcase, use cases, testimonials, pricing, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Art Direction System
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a distinctive multi-section concept with tight creative controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 12 horizontal section images for a creative studio website. Use editorial composition, one consistent brand world, varied backgrounds, and a clear conversion path.

output_examples.input: Design a hero section for an AI startup.
output_examples.output: One horizontal hero reference with a mid editorial scale, bottom-left text over a controlled full-bleed visual, and one clear CTA.

output: A consistent ink, paper, and accent palette.
Varied compositions across hero, trust, features, showcase, use cases, testimonials, pricing, and CTA.

output_examples.input: Make a creative agency landing page with 12 sections.
output_examples.output: Twelve separate section images with editorial pacing, off-grid moments, full-bleed showcase imagery, and a minimalist closing CTA.

best_practices: Provide the site type, target audience, brand mood, and desired section count when possible.
Name any required sections, products, assets, or conversion goals before generating images.
Review the full image set for palette consistency and section-by-section build clarity.
anti_patterns: Do not request a full website as one tall image if each section must be buildable.
Do not combine too many unrelated styles, industries, or palettes in one brief.
Do not treat the generated references as final accessibility or production validation.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: No. It generates image direction and visual references for frontend work.

faq.question: How many images will it produce for a landing page?
faq.answer: If no count is given, it defaults to 6 horizontal images, one for each section.

faq.question: Can I request a single hero image?
faq.answer: Yes. A hero-only request produces one horizontal hero section image.

faq.question: Is it only for SaaS websites?
faq.answer: No. It includes guidance for SaaS, products, e-commerce, agencies, portfolios, editorial sites, and luxury briefs.

faq.question: Will all sections use the same visual style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill enforces a consistent palette, typography system, CTA family, and image treatment across section images.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include the site type, audience, brand mood, section count, required content, and any visual constraints.
